page: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and integer operations.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and compute-intensive tasks. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core execution, image processing, and text processing. Large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks place the instance in the top 25% of tested hardware, reaching 178.37 GB/sec and 83.61 GB/sec respectively. Additionally, the hardware-assisted cryptography and compression features deliver strong throughput, as evidenced by a Passmark encryption score of 36252.74 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.25 GB/sec.

For specialized workloads, the c8i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in lightweight LLM inference, processing 2394.76 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model. It also handles high-throughput network tasks efficiently, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps and a static web serving throughput of 56.69 GB/sec. However, the instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff.

This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and advanced vector or matrix instructions. Suitable applications include software compilation, image and text processing pipelines, Redis-based caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning inference. The high-density DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation provide a predictable execution environment for multi-threaded decompression and physics simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz and a 480 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench tests, image processing, and text processing, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage. It is designed for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, cryptography, Redis caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A compute-optimized processor architecture featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated vector and integer calculations.

64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
